agencies.name: SDMX
agencies.description: SDMX is an initiative to foster standards for the exchange of statistical information. It is sponsored by the Bank for International Settlements (BIS), the European Central Bank (ECB), the Statistical Office of the European Union (Eurostat), the International Monetary Fund (IMF), the Organization for Economic Co-Operation and Development (OECD), the United Nations (UN) and the World Bank.

descriptions.en: SDMX is an initiative to foster standards for the exchange of statistical information. It is sponsored by the Bank for International Settlements (BIS), the European Central Bank (ECB), the Statistical Office of the European Union (Eurostat), the International Monetary Fund (IMF), the Organization for Economic Co-Operation and Development (OECD), the United Nations (UN) and the World Bank.

agencies.name: International Monetary Fund (IMF)
agencies.description: The IMF works to foster global growth and economic stability. It provides policy advice and financing to members in economic difficulties and also works with developing nations to help them achieve macroeconomic stability and reduce poverty.

descriptions.en: The IMF works to foster global growth and economic stability. It provides policy advice and financing to members in economic difficulties and also works with developing nations to help them achieve macroeconomic stability and reduce poverty.

codes.name: All reporting banks/institutions (domestic, foreign, consortium and unclassified)

names.en: All reporting banks/institutions (domestic, foreign, consortium and unclassified)

codes.name: Foreign branches
codes.description: Unincorporated entity wholly owned by another entity.

descriptions.en: Unincorporated entity wholly owned by another entity.

codes.name: Domestic banks
codes.description: Bank whose controlling parent is located in the respective BIS reporting country - for example, a bank with a controlling parent located in the United States is a US domestic bank.

descriptions.en: Bank whose controlling parent is located in the respective BIS reporting country - for example, a bank with a controlling parent located in the United States is a US domestic bank.

codes.name: Foreign subsidiaries
codes.description: A separately incorporated entity in which another entity has a majority or full participation.

descriptions.en: A separately incorporated entity in which another entity has a majority or full participation.

codes.name: Consortium and unclassified
codes.description: A consortium bank is a bank owned by two or more entities, in which no single entity has a controlling interest.

descriptions.en: A consortium bank is a bank owned by two or more entities, in which no single entity has a controlling interest.

codes.name: Domestic currency (ie currency of bank location country)

names.en: Domestic currency (ie currency of bank location country)

codes.description: The domestic currency refers to the currency that is legal tender in the reporting country, which is typically the currency issued by the reporting country’s central bank or monetary authority. In the LBS, the terms “domestic currency” and “local currency” are used interchangeably. By contrast, in the CBS the domestic currency is not the same as the local currency. In the CBS, local currency refers to the currency of the country where the counterparty resides.

descriptions.en: The domestic currency refers to the currency that is legal tender in the reporting country, which is typically the currency issued by the reporting country’s central bank or monetary authority. In the LBS, the terms “domestic currency” and “local currency” are used interchangeably. By contrast, in the CBS the domestic currency is not the same as the local currency. In the CBS, local currency refers to the currency of the country where the counterparty resides.

codes.name: Foreign currency (ie currencies foreign to bank location country)

names.en: Foreign currency (ie currencies foreign to bank location country)

codes.description: Currencies other than domestic currency (or local currency) are foreign currencies.

descriptions.en: Currencies other than domestic currency (or local currency) are foreign currencies.

codes.name: Debt securities
codes.description: Negotiable instrument serving as evidence of a debt. Debt securities include the following instruments: bills, bonds, notes, negotiable certificates of deposit, commercial paper, debentures, asset-backed securities, money market instruments and similar instruments normally traded in financial markets.

descriptions.en: Negotiable instrument serving as evidence of a debt. Debt securities include the following instruments: bills, bonds, notes, negotiable certificates of deposit, commercial paper, debentures, asset-backed securities, money market instruments and similar instruments normally traded in financial markets.

codes.name: Loans and deposits
codes.description: Non-negotiable debt instruments that are created when a creditor lends funds directly to a debtor. In the LBS, no distinction is made between loans and deposits; they are treated as economically equivalent. Loans and deposits include the cash leg of securities repurchase agreements, working capital and inter-office business.

descriptions.en: Non-negotiable debt instruments that are created when a creditor lends funds directly to a debtor. In the LBS, no distinction is made between loans and deposits; they are treated as economically equivalent. Loans and deposits include the cash leg of securities repurchase agreements, working capital and inter-office business.

codes.name: Derivatives
codes.description: Instrument whose value depends on some underlying financial asset, commodity or predefined variable.

descriptions.en: Instrument whose value depends on some underlying financial asset, commodity or predefined variable.

codes.name: Local claims
codes.description: Local claims are claims on counterparties located in the same country as the banking group’s entity that books the position.

descriptions.en: Local claims are claims on counterparties located in the same country as the banking group’s entity that books the position.

codes.name: Total claims
codes.description: A claim is a financial asset that has a counterpart liability. In the CBS, claims exclude financial derivatives. See also "Financial asset".

descriptions.en: A claim is a financial asset that has a counterpart liability. In the CBS, claims exclude financial derivatives. See also "Financial asset".

codes.name: Cross-border claims
codes.description: Cross-border claims are positions on counterparties located outside the country where the entity that books the position is located.

descriptions.en: Cross-border claims are positions on counterparties located outside the country where the entity that books the position is located.

codes.name: Total assets (financial and non-financial)
codes.description: Sum of financial assets and non-financial assets.

descriptions.en: Sum of financial assets and non-financial assets.

codes.name: International claims
codes.description: Claim on a non-resident or denominated in a foreign currency. International claims comprise cross-border claims in any currency plus local claims of foreign affiliates denominated in non-local currencies.

descriptions.en: Claim on a non-resident or denominated in a foreign currency. International claims comprise cross-border claims in any currency plus local claims of foreign affiliates denominated in non-local currencies.

codes.name: Claims on banks with head offices outside host country

names.en: Claims on banks with head offices outside host country

codes.name: Capital / equity
codes.description: Includes common shares, preferred shares, retained earning

descriptions.en: Includes common shares, preferred shares, retained earning

codes.name: Local liabilities
codes.description: Local liabilities are liabilities on counterparties located in the same country as the banking group’s entity that books the position.

descriptions.en: Local liabilities are liabilities on counterparties located in the same country as the banking group’s entity that books the position.

codes.name: Net positions
codes.description: Claims minus liabilities

descriptions.en: Claims minus liabilities

codes.name: Foreign Claims
codes.description: Claim on residents of countries other than the country where the controlling parent is located, ie a claim of a domestic bank on non-residents of the reporting country. Foreign claims comprise local claims of the bank's offices abroad as well as crossborder claims of the bank's offices worldwide.

descriptions.en: Claim on residents of countries other than the country where the controlling parent is located, ie a claim of a domestic bank on non-residents of the reporting country. Foreign claims comprise local claims of the bank's offices abroad as well as crossborder claims of the bank's offices worldwide.

codes.name: Guarantees extended
codes.description: Contingent liabilities that arise from an irrevocable obligation to pay a third-party beneficiary when a client fails to perform certain contractual obligations. Guarantees extended include the notional value of credit protection sold.

descriptions.en: Contingent liabilities that arise from an irrevocable obligation to pay a third-party beneficiary when a client fails to perform certain contractual obligations. Guarantees extended include the notional value of credit protection sold.

codes.name: Credit commitments
codes.description: Promise by a creditor to lend up to a specified amount to a borrower on demand. In the CBS, credit commitments refer to commitments that are irrevocable unilaterally by the creditor, ie revocable only with the consent of the borrower.

descriptions.en: Promise by a creditor to lend up to a specified amount to a borrower on demand. In the CBS, credit commitments refer to commitments that are irrevocable unilaterally by the creditor, ie revocable only with the consent of the borrower.

codes.name: Other potential exposures
codes.description: Sum of derivatives contracts, guarantees extended and credit commitments

descriptions.en: Sum of derivatives contracts, guarantees extended and credit commitments

codes.name: Cross-border
codes.description: Position on a non-resident - for example, claim on or liability to a counterparty located in a country other than the country where the banking office that books the position is located.

descriptions.en: Position on a non-resident - for example, claim on or liability to a counterparty located in a country other than the country where the banking office that books the position is located.

codes.name: Local
codes.description: Claim on or liability to a counterparty located in the same country as the banking office that books the position. Opposite of a "cross-border position".

descriptions.en: Claim on or liability to a counterparty located in the same country as the banking office that books the position. Opposite of a "cross-border position".

N:Not applicable
D:SFTs for which the collateral position type breakdown does not apply or is not available

texts.en:
N:Not applicable
D:SFTs for which the collateral position type breakdown does not apply or is not available

codes.name: Not applicable
codes.description: SFTs for which the collateral position type breakdown does not apply or is not available

descriptions.en: SFTs for which the collateral position type breakdown does not apply or is not available

codes.name: Banks, total
codes.description: Business between banks. In the LBS, "interbank" typically refers to business between banking offices and thus includes inter-office business.

descriptions.en: Business between banks. In the LBS, "interbank" typically refers to business between banking offices and thus includes inter-office business.

codes.name: Non-financial corporations
codes.description: Entity whose principal activity is the production of market goods or non-financial services. Non-financial corporations include the following entities: legally constituted corporations, branches of non-resident enterprises, quasi-corporations, notional resident units owning land, and resident non-profit institutions that are market producers of goods or non-financial services.

descriptions.en: Entity whose principal activity is the production of market goods or non-financial services. Non-financial corporations include the following entities: legally constituted corporations, branches of non-resident enterprises, quasi-corporations, notional resident units owning land, and resident non-profit institutions that are market producers of goods or non-financial services.

codes.name: Non-bank financial institutions
codes.description: Financial institution, other than a bank, engaged primarily in the provision of financial services and activities auxiliary to financial intermediation, such as fund management. Non-bank financial corporations include the following entities: special purpose vehicles, hedge funds, securities brokers, money market funds, pension funds, insurance companies, financial leasing corporations, CCPs, unit trusts, other financial auxiliaries and other captive financial institutions. They also include any public financial institutions such as development banks and export credit agencies. See "Non-bank financial corporation".

descriptions.en: Financial institution, other than a bank, engaged primarily in the provision of financial services and activities auxiliary to financial intermediation, such as fund management. Non-bank financial corporations include the following entities: special purpose vehicles, hedge funds, securities brokers, money market funds, pension funds, insurance companies, financial leasing corporations, CCPs, unit trusts, other financial auxiliaries and other captive financial institutions. They also include any public financial institutions such as development banks and export credit agencies. See "Non-bank financial corporation".

codes.name: General government
codes.description: Sectoral classification that refers collectively to the central government, state government, local government and social security funds. General government excludes the central bank and publicly owned corporations.

descriptions.en: Sectoral classification that refers collectively to the central government, state government, local government and social security funds. General government excludes the central bank and publicly owned corporations.

codes.name: Households and NPISHs
codes.description: Group of persons who share the same living accommodation, who pool some or all of their income and wealth, and who consume certain types of goods and services collectively, mainly housing and food. In the LBS and CBS, the household sector refers collectively to households and non-profit institutions serving households.

descriptions.en: Group of persons who share the same living accommodation, who pool some or all of their income and wealth, and who consume certain types of goods and services collectively, mainly housing and food. In the LBS and CBS, the household sector refers collectively to households and non-profit institutions serving households.

codes.name: Banks, related offices
codes.description: Entities that are part of the same banking group, ie that are within the perimeter of consolidation of the controlling parent institution. Includes the controlling parent institution, the head office of the bank (if different), and branches or subsidiaries that are part of the consolidated reporting entity.

descriptions.en: Entities that are part of the same banking group, ie that are within the perimeter of consolidation of the controlling parent institution. Includes the controlling parent institution, the head office of the bank (if different), and branches or subsidiaries that are part of the consolidated reporting entity.

codes.name: Banks, unrelated banks
codes.description: Entities that stay outside a banking group (ie do not have the same consolidated reporting entity).

descriptions.en: Entities that stay outside a banking group (ie do not have the same consolidated reporting entity).

codes.name: Banks, central banks
codes.description: Central banks; currency boards or independent currency authorities that issue national currency that is fully backed by foreign exchange reserves; government-affiliated agencies that are separate institutional units and primarily perform central bank activities; and international organisations that undertake activities similar to those of central banks.

descriptions.en: Central banks; currency boards or independent currency authorities that issue national currency that is fully backed by foreign exchange reserves; government-affiliated agencies that are separate institutional units and primarily perform central bank activities; and international organisations that undertake activities similar to those of central banks.

codes.name: Non-banks, total
codes.description: Entity that is not a bank. Sectoral classification that refers collectively to non-bank financial corporations and the non-financial sector.

descriptions.en: Entity that is not a bank. Sectoral classification that refers collectively to non-bank financial corporations and the non-financial sector.

codes.name: Official sector
codes.description: Sectoral classification used in the CBS that refers collectively to general government, central banks and international organisations.

descriptions.en: Sectoral classification used in the CBS that refers collectively to general government, central banks and international organisations.

codes.name: Non-financial sectors
codes.description: Sectoral classification that refers collectively to non-financial corporations, general government and households.

descriptions.en: Sectoral classification that refers collectively to non-financial corporations, general government and households.

codes.name: Non-bank private sector
codes.description: Sectoral classification used in CBS that refers collectively to non-bank financial corporations, non-financial corporations and households, ie the non-bank sector excluding general government.

descriptions.en: Sectoral classification used in CBS that refers collectively to non-bank financial corporations, non-financial corporations and households, ie the non-bank sector excluding general government.

codes.name: Non-financial private sector
codes.description: Sectoral classification that refers collectively to non-financial corporations and households, ie the non-financial sector excluding general government.

descriptions.en: Sectoral classification that refers collectively to non-financial corporations and households, ie the non-financial sector excluding general government.

codes.name: Missing value; data exist but were not collected

names.en: Missing value; data exist but were not collected

codes.name: FX and break adjusted change (BIS calculated)

names.en: FX and break adjusted change (BIS calculated)

codes.description: Change in amount outstanding between two points in time after the impact of methodological changes and exchange rate movements has been eliminated. The adjusted change approximates the flow between two points in time. In the LBS, the adjusted change is calculated by first converting US dollar-equivalent amounts outstanding into their original currency using end-of-period exchange rates, then calculating the difference in amounts outstanding in the original currency, and finally converting the difference into a US dollar-equivalent change using average period exchange rates.

descriptions.en: Change in amount outstanding between two points in time after the impact of methodological changes and exchange rate movements has been eliminated. The adjusted change approximates the flow between two points in time. In the LBS, the adjusted change is calculated by first converting US dollar-equivalent amounts outstanding into their original currency using end-of-period exchange rates, then calculating the difference in amounts outstanding in the original currency, and finally converting the difference into a US dollar-equivalent change using average period exchange rates.

codes.name: Amounts outstanding / Stocks
codes.description: Value of an asset or liability at a point in time.

descriptions.en: Value of an asset or liability at a point in time.

codes.name: Break in stocks
codes.description: Changes in assets and liabilities between opening and closing balance sheets that are due neither to transactions between institutional units nor to changes in value. (MFSM, 193)

descriptions.en: Changes in assets and liabilities between opening and closing balance sheets that are due neither to transactions between institutional units nor to changes in value. (MFSM, 193)

codes.name: Annual growth (BIS calculated)
codes.description: In the LBS and in the GLI, quarterly growth rates compounded over four quarters.

descriptions.en: In the LBS and in the GLI, quarterly growth rates compounded over four quarters.

codelists.name: Submission channel
codelists.description: This codelist contains values for the different submission channels

descriptions.en: This codelist contains values for the different submission channels
